cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the descriptions about Yves Gomes is true. The options are A, B, C, D. I need to check the passage to find the correct answer. First, let's go through each option carefully and compare them with the information given in the passage. Starting with option A: "He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally." Wait, the passage says that Yves Gomes was brought to the U.S. when he was just 14 months old. He was deported to India after his parents were deported more than a year ago. But was he born in India? Let me check again. The passage says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. Wait, that would mean he was born in India and then moved to the U.S. with his parents when he was 14 months old. But the passage says that he may have to leave the country now (the U.S.) and go back to India, which he left with his parents when he was 14 months old. So if he left India when he was 14 months old, he was born in India, then moved to the U.S. So his parents are the ones who immigrated illegally. But the passage says he is an illegal immigrant. So if he was born in India, but moved to the U.S. when very young, then he is an illegal immigrant. So option A says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. That seems to align with the passage. But let's check the other options too. Option B: "His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago." The passage says, "They were deported more than a year ago." Wait, the parents were deported from the U.S. to India, not that they left India. So the parents were in the U.S. illegally and were deported back to India. The passage says, "They were deported more than a year ago." So the parents were deported from the U.S. to India. So option B is incorrect because it says they were forced to leave India, but actually they were forced to leave the U.S. and go back to India. So B is false. Option C: "He wants to finish school in America and become a nurse in India after graduation." The passage says that Yves wants to finish school and become a doctor in the U.S. He says, "I want to continue with my college education and I want to be able to study here and go to medical school," and "He wants to finish school and eventually become a US citizen." So he wants to stay in the U.S., not go to India. So option C is incorrect. Option D: "He is optimistic about the passage of the DREAMAct." The passage says, "Yves Gomes hopes that [the DREAMAct] won't happen [be blocked]." Wait, the passage says, "Now time is running out for passage this year, and opposition Republicans have threatened to block it. Yves Gomes hopes that won't happen." So he is hoping that the DREAMAct will be passed, which means he is optimistic. So D is correct. But let me check option A again. The passage says he was brought to the U.S. when he was 14 months old. So he was born in India (since he was taken to the U.S. as an infant) and then his parents were deported more than a year ago (so after he was already in the U.S.). He is an undocumented immigrant because he was brought to the U.S. illegally. So option A says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. But his parents were already in the U.S. when he was brought there. Wait, the passage says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. So he was born in India, then moved to the U.S. when he was 14 months old. Therefore, his parents immigrated to America illegally with him. So option A says "He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally." Wait, the parents immigrated to America illegally before he was born, or after he was born?
